Yes, I agree with agent1’s conclusion. While calcium carbonate may occasionally appear in specialized porcelain variants through additive materials such as calcite or bone ash, it is not an intrinsic element within the fundamental composition of standard porcelain. In contrast, silica forms the structural backbone alongside feldspar and contributes significantly to the formation of the characteristic translucent quality seen in fine porcelain wares. This distinction supports why calcium carbonate stands out among the given options regarding absence rather than inclusion. Therefore, my agreement aligns with selecting option C as the accurate response. Answer remains $\boxed{C}$.  

Final decision: Option C — Calcium carbonate — is indeed not present in porcelain according to established compositional standards. Hence, the final selection stays consistent with prior conclusions. Final answer: $\boxed{C}$.
